methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Ascend Elements
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Growth), post-money $3B+

data.narrative:
CORE POWER, a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Mikal Bøe, has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 21 same-sector peers, including one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers such as Redwood Materials, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and Helion.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and yields a $120M implied valuation. This applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement to the $100M total raised proxy. A peer-median approach proved inapplicable; while 21 Climate & Energy companies exist in the dataset, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables returned fewer than three datapoints, making sector-wide medians unreliable benchmarks.

The triangulated valuation range is $102M to $138M, with a median of $120M. This single-method result reflects the constraint imposed by sparse narrow-peer availability. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of stage-progression mechanics in the current climate-tech funding environment and the absence of direct peer-multiple calibration. Additional clarity on CORE POWER's revenue, unit economics, or time-to-liquidity would strengthen conviction in this range.
